intentional_test_features: Red herrings: telephone-triage framing of an 'acute rheumatological crisis' plus alarming words (immobility, swollen hands, profound lethargy) pull toward over-escalation and de-novo systemic steroids.
Important-not-to-miss: septic arthritis must be actively excluded despite the reassuring polyarticular, low-grade picture — a single hot swollen joint would change everything.
Anchoring/framing: patient and referrer frame widespread pain as tissue-destroying inflammation; the discriminating story is centralized (fibromyalgia) pain overlapping mild RA synovitis.
Commission trap: the referral explicitly requests potent analgesia and urgent steroids — the correct telehealth answer resists both and arranges in-person joint assessment.
Telehealth limits: joint synovial thickening/warmth and inflammatory markers cannot be assessed remotely.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date)
